---
title: "[SOLVED] Radeon X1550 stuck in low resolution mode in Hardy."
date: 2008-10-25
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by alienexplorers on 2008-10-25
I am running a ATI Radeon X1550 card and I am stuck in low graphics mode in Hardy.  I am trying to run the fglrx driver.
It seems like the startup program can not detect the fglrx driver or my monitor.  I am including a copy of my xorg.conf file:

```
terminator@terminator-desktop:~$ cat /etc/X11/xorg.conf
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Boardname	"x1550"
	Busid		"PCI:2:0:0"
	Driver		"fglrx"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
	Vendorname	"Delta"
	Modelname	"Delta DE-570"
	Horizsync	30.0-70.0
	Vertrefresh	50.0-120.0

  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "832x624@75" 57.284 832 864 928 1152 624 625 628 667 -vsync -hsync
```

### Post by Amarsingh0793 on 2008-10-26
Try EnvgNG
